gaps: 🧠 LIFECYCLE DIMENSION — Repeated dialog persistence: The bug involves the FMD location dialog appearing REPEATEDLY after 'Don't allow' is selected. None of the existing test cases verify what happens after the dialog is dismissed multiple times — specifically whether it stops reappearing after the first occurrence (step 7 of the bug). This is a lifecycle/persistence gap: the dialog's reappearance behavior across screen transitions, app backgrounding, or subsequent navigations is not tested.

🧠 STATE TRIGGER — Dialog reappearance trigger not isolated: The bug says the dialog appears repeatedly in 'main settings (Home → Device Settings)'. Existing tests (65293013, 65293014) cover initial denial and revocation scenarios, but none test the exact trigger: does the dialog reappear on every entry to Device Settings? On every app foreground? On every Bluetooth reconnect? The specific trigger condition for the repeated appearance is untested.

🧠 LIFECYCLE DIMENSION — Post-dismissal state verification: Step 7 says 'After the first occurrence, verify that the dialog no longer reappears.' No existing test verifies that after dismissing the dialog once (post-onboarding), navigating away and back to Device Settings does NOT trigger the dialog again. This is a critical persistence gap.

🧠 LIFECYCLE DIMENSION — App lifecycle interaction: No test covers the scenario where the user denies permission during onboarding, then backgrounds the app, reopens it, and checks whether the dialog reappears. The bug's repeated dialog could be triggered by app lifecycle events (onResume, onStart) that are not tested.

🧠 DEVICE ASYMMETRY — No device-specific validation: The bug scenario does not specify a device type, but Poly has multiple device families (Sync 20/20+, Free 60, Voyager 5200, Surround 85). The FMD feature may behave differently depending on whether the device supports Find My Device (e.g., some Poly devices lack FMD capability entirely). No test validates the dialog behavior across different Poly device types.

🧠 STATE TRIGGER — Missing test for dialog dismissal count/rate limiting: The bug implies the dialog appears 'repeatedly' but eventually stops. This suggests there may be a rate-limiting or dismissal-count mechanism. No test verifies how many times the dialog appears before it stops, or whether the count resets after app restart or phone reboot.

generated_testcases.title: FMD Dialog — Repeated denial: dialog stops reappearing after first dismissal on Device Settings entry
steps: Precondition: Fresh install. During onboarding, select 'Don't Allow' for FMD location permission.
Navigate to: Home → Device Settings
When FMD dialog appears, tap 'Don't Allow' (or system deny).
Navigate away: Home → Back to Device List
Navigate back: Device List → Device Settings
Observe whether FMD dialog reappears.
Repeat steps 4-5 two more times.
generated_testcases.expected: 🧠 BUG SIGNATURE: FMD dialog appears on first entry to Device Settings after onboarding denial. After dismissing it once, dialog MUST NOT reappear on subsequent entries to Device Settings. Actual (If Bug): Dialog reappears on every entry to Device Settings, never rate-limited.

generated_testcases.title: FMD Dialog — App backgrounding triggers re-dialog after onboarding denial
steps: Precondition: Fresh install. During onboarding, select 'Don't Allow' for FMD location permission.
Navigate to: Home → Device Settings
When FMD dialog appears, tap 'Don't Allow'.
Press Home button to background the app.
Wait 5 seconds, then reopen app from recent apps.
Observe whether FMD dialog reappears immediately or on any navigation.
Navigate: Device Settings → Back → Device Settings again.
Observe dialog behavior.
generated_testcases.expected: 🧠 BUG SIGNATURE: After onboarding denial and one dialog dismissal, backgrounding and foregrounding the app MUST NOT trigger the FMD dialog again — neither on resume nor on re-entry to Device Settings. Actual (If Bug): Dialog reappears on app foreground (onResume trigger) or on next Device Settings visit.

generated_testcases.title: FMD Dialog — Bluetooth disconnect/reconnect triggers re-dialog after onboarding denial
steps: Precondition: Fresh install. During onboarding, select 'Don't Allow' for FMD location permission.
Navigate to: Home → Device Settings
When FMD dialog appears, tap 'Don't Allow'.
Disconnect Bluetooth from phone Settings (or turn off headset).
Wait 10 seconds, reconnect Bluetooth.
Navigate: Device Settings (if not already there).
Observe whether FMD dialog reappears.
generated_testcases.expected: 🧠 BUG SIGNATURE: After onboarding denial and one dialog dismissal, a Bluetooth disconnect+reconnect cycle MUST NOT trigger the FMD dialog again on re-entry to Device Settings. Actual (If Bug): Dialog reappears after Bluetooth state transition, indicating the permission denial state is not persisted across reconnections.

generated_testcases.title: FMD Dialog — App force-kill and relaunch: dialog does not reappear after onboarding denial
steps: Precondition: Fresh install. During onboarding, select 'Don't Allow' for FMD location permission.
Navigate to: Home → Device Settings
When FMD dialog appears, tap 'Don't Allow'.
Force-kill the app (swipe from recent apps).
Relaunch app and reconnect to device.
Navigate to: Home → Device Settings
Observe whether FMD dialog reappears.
generated_testcases.expected: 🧠 BUG SIGNATURE: After onboarding denial and one dialog dismissal, force-killing and relaunching the app MUST NOT trigger the FMD dialog again on re-entry to Device Settings. Actual (If Bug): Dialog reappears after app restart, indicating the 'don't show again' flag is not persisted to disk.

generated_testcases.title: FMD Dialog — Phone reboot: dialog does not reappear after onboarding denial
steps: Precondition: Fresh install. During onboarding, select 'Don't Allow' for FMD location permission.
Navigate to: Home → Device Settings
When FMD dialog appears, tap 'Don't Allow'.
Reboot the phone.
After reboot, launch app and reconnect to device.
Navigate to: Home → Device Settings
Observe whether FMD dialog reappears.
generated_testcases.expected: 🧠 BUG SIGNATURE: After onboarding denial and one dialog dismissal, a full phone reboot MUST NOT trigger the FMD dialog again on re-entry to Device Settings. Actual (If Bug): Dialog reappears after reboot, indicating the dismissal preference is not persisted across device reboots.

generated_testcases.title: FMD Dialog — Dismissal count/rate-limiting: verify dialog stops after N dismissals
steps: Precondition: Fresh install. During onboarding, select 'Don't Allow' for FMD location permission.
Navigate to: Home → Device Settings
When FMD dialog appears, tap 'Don't Allow'. Count = 1.
Navigate: Device Settings → Back → Device Settings
If dialog reappears, tap 'Don't Allow'. Count = 2.
Repeat step 4 until dialog no longer appears on entry.
Record total dismissal count (N) before dialog stops.
Force-kill app, relaunch, reconnect, navigate to Device Settings.
Observe whether dialog reappears (count reset check).
generated_testcases.expected: 🧠 BUG SIGNATURE: The FMD dialog must stop reappearing after a finite number of dismissals (expected: 1). Record actual N. After force-kill and relaunch, the dialog MUST NOT reappear (count must persist). Actual (If Bug): Dialog reappears indefinitely (no rate limit) OR count resets after app restart.

generated_testcases.title: FMD Dialog — Device asymmetry: behavior on FMD-capable vs non-FMD device
steps: Precondition: Two devices paired — Device A (FMD-capable, e.g., Voyager 5200 series) and Device B (non-FMD, e.g., Sync 20 without FMD).
For Device A: Fresh install. During onboarding, select 'Don't Allow' for FMD location.
Navigate to: Home → Device Settings (Device A)
When FMD dialog appears, tap 'Don't Allow'.
Navigate away and back to Device Settings — verify no re-dialog.
Switch to Device B: Navigate to: Home → Device Settings (Device B)
Observe whether any FMD-related dialog appears for Device B.
Navigate away and back to Device Settings (Device B) — observe.
generated_testcases.expected: 🧠 DEVICE ASYMMETRY: For FMD-capable device (A): dialog appears once post-onboarding denial, then stops. For non-FMD device (B): NO FMD dialog should ever appear, even on first entry to Device Settings. Actual (If Bug): Dialog appears for non-FMD device (incorrect) OR dialog reappears repeatedly for FMD-capable device.

generated_testcases.title: FMD Dialog — Immediate state trigger: kill app mid-dialog before dismissal
steps: Precondition: Fresh install. During onboarding, select 'Don't Allow' for FMD location permission.
Navigate to: Home → Device Settings
FMD dialog appears on screen. DO NOT tap any button.
Force-kill the app immediately while dialog is visible.
Relaunch app and reconnect to device.
Navigate to: Home → Device Settings
Observe whether FMD dialog appears.
generated_testcases.expected: 🧠 IMMEDIATE STATE TRIGGER: If the app is killed while the FMD dialog is showing (before any dismissal action), the dialog MUST reappear on next Device Settings entry (state was never committed). Actual (If Bug): Dialog does not reappear (incorrectly treats unhandled dialog as dismissed) OR dialog reappears indefinitely (no state tracking).

generated_testcases.title: FMD Dialog — System-level permission revocation after onboarding denial
steps: Precondition: Fresh install. During onboarding, select 'Don't Allow' for FMD location permission.
Navigate to: Home → Device Settings
When FMD dialog appears, tap 'Don't Allow'.
Verify dialog does not reappear on re-entry.
Go to phone Settings → Apps → Poly Studio Mobile → Permissions
Manually grant Location permission (if denied).
Return to app, navigate to: Home → Device Settings
Observe whether FMD dialog appears now that system permission is granted.
Navigate away and back — observe re-dialog behavior.
generated_testcases.expected: 🧠 BUG SIGNATURE: After onboarding denial and one dialog dismissal, if user later manually grants Location via system Settings, the FMD dialog SHOULD appear once on next Device Settings entry (since permission state changed). After dismissing it, it must NOT reappear. Actual (If Bug): Dialog does not appear after manual grant (missed opportunity to enable FMD) OR dialog reappears indefinitely after grant.

generated_testcases.title: FMD Dialog — Onboarding 'Allow' then later system revocation triggers dialog
steps: Precondition: Fresh install. During onboarding, select 'Allow' for FMD location permission.
Complete setup, navigate to: Home → Device Settings
Verify no FMD dialog appears (already granted).
Go to phone Settings → Apps → Poly Studio Mobile → Permissions
Revoke Location permission.
Return to app, navigate to: Home → Device Settings
Observe whether FMD dialog appears (should prompt to re-enable).
Tap 'Don't Allow' on the dialog.
Navigate away and back to Device Settings — observe re-dialog.
generated_testcases.expected: 🧠 BUG SIGNATURE: After system-level revocation of a previously-granted permission, the FMD dialog MUST appear once on next Device Settings entry. After dismissing with 'Don't Allow', it must NOT reappear on subsequent entries. Actual (If Bug): Dialog does not appear after system revocation (silent failure) OR dialog reappears repeatedly after dismissal.
